name: L-SDR-U2™
description: A USRP2-class SDR motherboard designed for higher-throughput, FPGA-based, and networked SDR applications. Board-only platform intended for evaluation, lab use, or replacement in existing USRP2/N210-style systems. Compatible with established SDR software stacks including GNU Radio.
priceNote: Indicative single-unit / evaluation pricing. Volume and enterprise terms via RFQ.

name: L-SDR-U1™
description: A USRP1-compatible SDR motherboard designed for continuity with legacy GNU Radio workflows. Suitable for research, education, and long-lived field systems built around the original USRP1 ecosystem. Board-only configuration.
priceNote: Indicative single-unit / evaluation pricing. Volume and enterprise terms via RFQ.

name: RF900™
description: A 900 MHz RF daughterboard compatible with the RFX900 design lineage. Intended for sub-1 GHz wireless applications including ISM-band communications, telemetry, and legacy SDR deployments requiring stable RF performance.
priceNote: Indicative single-unit pricing. Quantity, QA scope, and calibration options via RFQ.

name: URAN-1
description: A compact SDR unit derived from the USRP1-compatible platform family. Optimized for embedded integration and system-level deployment where size, simplicity, and legacy software compatibility are priorities.
priceNote: Indicative single-unit pricing. Not intended for bulk or enterprise deployment without RFQ.

name: L-SDR-N210™
description: A USRP-N210-class SDR motherboard providing higher data throughput, FPGA-based processing, and Ethernet-connected SDR operation. Board-only replacement or evaluation unit compatible with USRP2/N210-style system architectures.
priceNote: Indicative single-unit / replacement pricing. Enterprise, volume, and QA requirements via RFQ.
